description: Cross-dataset evaluation questions for SC-007 (75% accuracy requirement)

questions.question: Which customers have placed orders?
questions.expected_join: customers INNER JOIN orders ON customers.customer_id = orders.customer_id

questions.question: What products have been ordered?
questions.expected_join: orders INNER JOIN products ON orders.product_id = products.product_id

questions.question: Which customers have the highest order totals?
questions.expected_join: customers INNER JOIN orders ON customers.customer_id = orders.customer_id
questions.expected_aggregation: SUM(total_amount) GROUP BY customer_name ORDER BY DESC

questions.question: How many orders has each customer placed?
questions.expected_join: customers INNER JOIN orders ON customers.customer_id = orders.customer_id

questions.question: What products did Alice Johnson order?
questions.expected_join: customers INNER JOIN orders ON customers.customer_id = orders.customer_id INNER JOIN products ON orders.product_id = products.product_id

questions.question: Which customers ordered electronics products?
questions.expected_join: customers INNER JOIN orders ON customers.customer_id = orders.customer_id INNER JOIN products ON orders.product_id = products.product_id

questions.question: What is the total revenue for each product?
questions.expected_join: orders INNER JOIN products ON orders.product_id = products.product_id

questions.question: Which California customers have placed orders?
questions.expected_join: customers INNER JOIN orders ON customers.customer_id = orders.customer_id

questions.question: What orders were placed in January 2024?
questions.expected_join: customers INNER JOIN orders ON customers.customer_id = orders.customer_id

questions.question: What is the total quantity of each product ordered by each customer?
questions.expected_join: customers INNER JOIN orders ON customers.customer_id = orders.customer_id INNER JOIN products ON orders.product_id = products.product_id

questions.question: List all orders sorted by total amount
questions.expected_join: customers INNER JOIN orders ON customers.customer_id = orders.customer_id

questions.question: How many different products has each customer ordered?
questions.expected_join: customers INNER JOIN orders ON customers.customer_id = orders.customer_id

questions.question: What is the average order value for each customer?
questions.expected_join: customers INNER JOIN orders ON customers.customer_id = orders.customer_id

questions.question: Which customers in California ordered Widget A?
questions.expected_join: customers INNER JOIN orders ON customers.customer_id = orders.customer_id INNER JOIN products ON orders.product_id = products.product_id

questions.question: What is the largest order placed by each customer?
questions.expected_join: customers INNER JOIN orders ON customers.customer_id = orders.customer_id

questions.question: What is the total revenue by product category?
questions.expected_join: orders INNER JOIN products ON orders.product_id = products.product_id

questions.question: Show the top 3 customers by total order value
questions.expected_join: customers INNER JOIN orders ON customers.customer_id = orders.customer_id
questions.expected_aggregation: SUM(total_amount) GROUP BY customer_name ORDER BY DESC LIMIT 3

questions.question: How many orders were placed each day?
questions.expected_join: None (single table, but related to multi-dataset context)

questions.question: Which customers ordered products with a unit price above $100?
questions.expected_join: customers INNER JOIN orders ON customers.customer_id = orders.customer_id INNER JOIN products ON orders.product_id = products.product_id

questions.question: For each customer, show total orders, total quantity, and total amount
questions.expected_join: customers INNER JOIN orders ON customers.customer_id = orders.customer_id
questions.expected_aggregation: COUNT(*), SUM(quantity), SUM(total_amount) GROUP BY customer_name

evaluation_notes: Questions are designed to test automatic JOIN detection and generation across 2-3 related datasets. System must identify relationships via cross_references table and generate appropriate SQL with JOINs.
